sourceMovie.title: Excalibur
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Excalibur (1981) as a adventure theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The adventure and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Excalibur clicked for you, The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and fantasy, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Excalibur?
faq.answer: Try The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring, The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King, The Lord of the Rings: The Two Towers—each shares DNA with Excalibur while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Excalibur?
faq.answer: Excalibur (1981) is best described as Adventure, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Excalibur worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this adventure lane.
